D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Photonic Crystal Bandgap — Periodic Dielectric Stop Band

A 1D quarter-wave stack reflects ~100% in a frequency band centred at λ/4 layer thickness; same physics as semiconductor bandgaps applied to light

Yablonovitch (1987) coined "photonic crystal". A Bragg mirror (1D PhC) of alternating n_H/n_L layers each λ_0/(4n) thick gives bandgap width Δλ/λ₀ ≈ (4/π) arcsin((n_H − n_L)/(n_H + n_L)). 2D and 3D PhCs (opal, inverse opal) give omnidirectional gaps — basis of photonic-bandgap fibres.

Slide layer-pair count to see gap deepen and edges sharpen.
